other_observers: Charles Hundertmark, Buzz Schaumberg, Mary Cay Burger; I don't know who originally found it, but our DFO group found it including people listed above.
specific_location: S end of Cherry Creek Reservoir
description: Three NECOs were seen among Double-crested Cormorants on DFO trip to Cherry Creek Reservoir, Arapahoe Co., CO on 22 Oct 2011 led by Buzz Schaumberg. Two NECOs were adults, 1 juv. I concentrated on the NECO sillouetted against nearby swimming DCCOs that I photographed with my Canon 60D w/ 100-400 mm IS Canon lens. Smaller, thinner than DCCO with smaller head and bill. Black plumage, longer tail than DCCO. White V between bill and face. This NECO perched on protruding old tree stump in Cherry Creek Res. Then it turned to perch the opposite direction on same stump. I took many photos, 3 of which are on my website www.kayniyo.com.Behaviors: This particular NECO was swimming with many DCCOs and climbed up on the stump protruding from the reservoir to perch in the sun. It was watchful and turned around to perch the other dircection providing another view for my lens.Call: None notedPlumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Kayleen A Niyo, Kayleen A Niyo, Kayleen A Niyo, Kayleen A Niyo
